content.problemStatement:
The nation of Islandia can be represented as an N√óNN \times NN√óN grid. Let (i,j)(i, j)(i,j) denote the square at the iii-th row from the top and the jjj-th column from the left. A cell (i,j)(i, j)(i,j) is labelled 111 if it is land and 000 if it is water. Any two cells of the grid which share a side, and are both land are said to belong to the same island.

Islandia holds two contests a year, the first at (xa,ya)(x_a, y_a)(xa‚Äč,ya‚Äč) and the second at (xb,yb)(x_b, y_b)(xb‚Äč,yb‚Äč). Ashish will need to travel from cell (xa,ya)(x_a, y_a)(xa‚Äč,ya‚Äč) to (xb,yb)(x_b, y_b)(xb‚Äč,yb‚Äč). To do so, he can perform a sequence of zero or more moves. In each move if he is at (xi,yi)(x_i, y_i)(xi‚Äč,yi‚Äč), he can perform one of two actions:


Type 1: Move from (xi,yi)(x_i, y_i)(xi‚Äč,yi‚Äč) to some land cell (xj,yj)(x_j, y_j)(xj‚Äč,yj‚Äč) that lies on a different island at a cost of ‚ą£xi‚ąíxj‚ą£+‚ą£yi‚ąíyj‚ą£| x_i - x_j | + | y_i - y_j |‚ą£xi‚Äč‚ąíxj‚Äč‚ą£+‚ą£yi‚Äč‚ąíyj‚Äč‚ą£
Type 2: Move from (xi,yi)(x_i, y_i)(xi‚Äč,yi‚Äč) to some land cell (xj,yj)(x_j, y_j)(xj‚Äč,yj‚Äč) that lies on the same island at a cost of 000.
Let the beauty of a sequence of moves be the number of moves of type 111 while traveling from (xa,ya)(x_a, y_a)(xa‚Äč,ya‚Äč) to (xb,yb)(x_b, y_b)(xb‚Äč,yb‚Äč). We define the simple cost as the minimum cost of a sequence with beauty‚Č§1\mathrm{beauty} \leq 1beauty‚Č§1.

Ashish wants to figure out the maximum beauty possible to go from (xa,ya)(x_a,y_a)(xa‚Äč,ya‚Äč) to (xb,yb)(x_b, y_b)(xb‚Äč,yb‚Äč) without exceeding the simple cost. However, Ashish only knows (xa,ya)(x_a, y_a)(xa‚Äč,ya‚Äč). Find the answer for **every possible** land cell (xb,yb)(x_b, y_b)(xb‚Äč,yb‚Äč).

Note that we **DO NOT need to minimize the cost of the trip**, only maximize the beauty without exceeding the simple cost.
constraints: 1‚Č§T‚Č§201 \leq T \leq 201‚Č§T‚Č§20
1‚Č§xa,ya‚Č§N1\le x_a, y_a\le N1‚Č§xa‚Äč,ya‚Äč‚Č§N
The sum of N2N^2N2 over all cases does not exceed 4‚čÖ1044\cdot 10^44‚čÖ104
###Subtasks
**Subtask #1 (15 points):**
N‚Č§50N \leq 50N‚Č§50
The sum of N2N^2N2 over all cases does not exceed 250025002500
**Subtask #2 (35 points):**
The sum of N2N^2N2 over all cases does not exceed 10410^4104
###Sample Input 1
`###Sample Output 1
`###Sample Input 2
`###Sample Output 2
`###Sample Input 3
`###Sample Output 3
`###Explanation
**Sample 1, test case 2:**

The simple cost from (2,4)(2, 4)(2,4) to (5,2)(5, 2)(5,2) is ‚ą£2‚ąí5‚ą£+‚ą£4‚ąí2‚ą£=3+2=5| 2 - 5 | + | 4 - 2 | = 3 + 2 = 5‚ą£2‚ąí5‚ą£+‚ą£4‚ąí2‚ą£=3+2=5. We can achieve a beauty of 222 without exceeding the simple cost in the following manner:
Perform a move of type 111 from (2,4)(2, 4)(2,4) to (5,4)(5, 4)(5,4) at a cost of 333.
Perform a move of type 111 from (5,4)(5, 4)(5,4) to (5,2)(5, 2)(5,2) at a cost of 222.
**Sample 3, test case 1:**

The simple cost from (1,1)(1, 1)(1,1) to (1,7)(1, 7)(1,7) is ‚ą£1‚ąí1‚ą£+‚ą£1‚ąí7‚ą£=0+6=6| 1 - 1 | + | 1 - 7 | = 0 + 6 = 6‚ą£1‚ąí1‚ą£+‚ą£1‚ąí7‚ą£=0+6=6. However we can achieve a beauty of 222 without exceeding the simple cost in the following manner:
Perform a move of type 111 from (1,1)(1, 1)(1,1) to (1,3)(1, 3)(1,3) at a cost of 222.
Perform a move of type 222 from (1,3)(1, 3)(1,3) to (1,5)(1, 5)(1,5) at a cost of 000.
Perform a move of type 111 from (1,5)(1, 5)(1,5) to (1,7)(1, 7)(1,7) at a cost of 222.
Note that the cost of some sequence with beauty >1> 1>1 might be lower than the simple cost.
**Sample 3, test case 2:**

In the second case of third sample, the simple cost from (9,9)(9, 9)(9,9) to (1,9)(1, 9)(1,9) is ‚ą£9‚ąí1‚ą£+‚ą£9‚ąí9‚ą£=8+0=8| 9 - 1 | + | 9 - 9 | = 8 + 0 = 8‚ą£9‚ąí1‚ą£+‚ą£9‚ąí9‚ą£=8+0=8. However we can achieve a beauty of 444 without exceeding the simple cost in the following manner:
Perform a move of type 111 from (9,9)(9, 9)(9,9) to (9,7)(9, 7)(9,7) at a cost of 222.
Perform a move of type 222 from (9,7)(9, 7)(9,7) to (1,7)(1, 7)(1,7) at a cost of 000.
Perform a move of type 111 from (1,7)(1, 7)(1,7) to (1,9)(1, 9)(1,9) at a cost of 222.
Perform a move of type 111 from (1,9)(1, 9)(1,9) to (1,7)(1, 7)(1,7) at a cost of 222.
Perform a move of type 111 from (1,7)(1, 7)(1,7) to (1,9)(1, 9)(1,9) at a cost of 222.

Also note that in the same sample the answer for (1,7)(1, 7)(1,7) will be 111, not 333 as the simple cost from (9,9)(9, 9)(9,9) to (1,7)(1, 7)(1,7) is 222. This can be achieved by performing a move of type 111 from (9,9)(9, 9)(9,9) to (9,7)(9, 7)(9,7) at cost of 222 and then a move of type 222 from (9,7)(9, 7)(9,7) to (1,7)(1, 7)(1,7) at a cost of 000.
content.inputFormat:
The first line contains an integer TTT, the number of test cases. Then TTT test cases follow.
The first line of each test case contains an integer NNN.
The following NNN lines each contains a string S=s1s2‚Ä¶sNS=s_1 s_2 \ldots s_NS=s1‚Äčs2‚Äč‚Ä¶sN‚Äč. The string on the iii-th line represents the iii-th row of the grid, where sjs_{j}sj‚Äč is 111 if the cell (i,j)(i, j)(i,j) is land, and 000 if it is water.
The last line of each test case contains two integers xax_{a}xa‚Äč and yay_{a}ya‚Äč where (xa,ya)(x_a, y_a)(xa‚Äč,ya‚Äč) represents the cell where the first contest will be held. It is guaranteed that this cell will be a land cell.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print NNN lines, each containing the NNN space-separated integers. The jjj-th value of the iii-th line represents the maximum beauty possible on a journey from (xa,ya)(x_a, y_a)(xa‚Äč,ya‚Äč) to (i,j)(i, j)(i,j) satisfying the mentioned conditions, if the cell (i,j)(i, j)(i,j) is land. If (i,j)(i, j)(i,j) is a water cell or belongs to the same island as (xa,ya)(x_a, y_a)(xa‚Äč,ya‚Äč), the value to be printed is 0.
